item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Concise, precise, terribly disquieting in its applications, and of course without any explanations to its origins. I miss the SCPs from those simpler times.
+1 of course

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This post is kinda meh. I think it would be a +0 from me if the 3rd addendum hadn't left a sour taste in my mouth. It's just so bare bones that it could have left out or, better yet, devoted to exploring another facet of this technology. More investigation of the shape-shifting abilities, some test logs involving a wider variety of test subjects, or a better explanation of the limits of this "programming language" are just a few things that could be filled in instead. -1 from me just in the hopes that the score will get down low enough for someone to actually do something about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, simple, and sweet. Just as most series 1 articles are, which is something I appreciate about the predecessors of this wiki.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
010, we will control them.
What bothers most people about this one is that it’s so simple, but it really doesn’t matter in my opinion. It gets it’s point across, gives us a cool skip, actually goes into detail a bit about how it works and what rules govern it (which is cool) and doesn’t overstay it’s welcome.
What I want to know is why no-one is talking about the fact that you can change people’s shape with this thing. Can I turn someone into a large fleshy dog and get him to eat his family? Yes, says this skip. Do the changes stay when you remove it? Can you change materials? Can you change the mass of your subject? I wish we had gotten a test log exploring this, instead of the replicability. Oh well.
And nice attention to detail with that transistor.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one really has some good potential for brilliant testing log narratives… Doubt it will ever see them written though.

item.title: Pondering
item.content:encoded:
I'm actually seriously impressed that these aren't used on the D class test subjects when preforming tests with/on other SCP's; maybe because most of the test researchers are wanting a more authentic test or are worried about losing one of SCP-010-2 through SCP-010-7? I just feel it would make the testing's process a lot easier for the researchers….

Or have them test to SCP-010 effects on SCP-014, SCP-073, possibly even SCP-076?

Maybe see if someone who has being controlled by SCP-010 were to hold SCP-093 if it would change to a new unidentified color?

I am also confused on why they didn't seem to do more testing on non-human subjects or at least if they had why not at least add in at least a sentence stating as such?

My reasoning's for more specifics on the animal testing is that, as the testing shows, they are able to recreate the generic outer workings for SCP-010-2 through SCP-010-07 so if they where able to make one large enough would SCP-010 be able to control SCP-682?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy hell. For something slotted so high in the mainlist, this sure doesn't have that great of a rating. It's pretty easy to see why: it's a straightforward item without any threads with which to build intrigue. This is in desperate need for a rewrite, or replacement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do the words on the collars say? Even a [DATA EXPUNGED] would help. But then this is my first comment so I'm happy to learn otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NO ITS NOT LIKE THAT NOOOOOOOOO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(mind control is actually one of my favorite tropes because of all the things you can do with it in stories)

( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°) I knew it was a fetish thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think it's a fetish thing.

It's a cliche as old as time itself, the mind control (mind control is actually one of my favorite tropes because of all the things you can do with it in stories)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems like a fetish thing… I think it's a fetish thing…

Regardless of whether or not this is a fetish thing, I'm unimpressed. Sorry. It's boring. I feel as though the brevity, the simplicity, doesn't work here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh another great written scp,liking it.

item.title: Requesting minor change
item.content:encoded:
"Cause of death appears to be mass suicide"

I think this should be changed into something like this:

"Cause of death appears to be __ during a mass suicide."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good idea; less than mediocre execution. -1

It has too little detail and not enough oomph, notably in the Special Containment Procedures. I mean come on, it's so short and uninteresting that it might as well not even be there.

item.title: Usage
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who thinks that this could have plenty tactical uses?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always like this one, but I'm wondering if a couple of experiment logs may add just a little more to this SCiP? Upvoted +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this article. It's concise, it's clear, and it's done quite logically. This is a very solid example of writing. +1, I hope this gets more attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the third addendum somewhat weakens this one, and it could be improved by expanding upon the ideas presented in the first addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 10-8 is the new one constructed entirely by the foundation. It does not function without certain "unreplicable" components from one of the original collars.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there's an error, in Tests 2 and 3, they mention 10-8 when their are 7 components (the remote ,1, and the collars, 2 - 7) Was that intentional, or overlooked?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I agree that it doesn't need anything added, I think that the first addendum could go into the description instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Dex. I think that this article benefits from its simplicity and brevity, and elaborating on it would only be to its detriment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always liked how brief this one is. It's straightforward and clear. The italics at the very end are an excellent finishing touch, and you don't see that particular style of researcher remark often.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
I'm placing this article up for review, it could use some more attention. Come check it out.

This would definitely need expansion if it wanted to be good, but I think the concept of something that can control people has been done better elsewhere, and this interpretation is just mediocre. Maybe if some drastic additions were made, it would be better, otherwise it's just… kinda meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This needs to expand upon and illustrate1 the concept if it needs to be any good.

Footnotes
1. Not literally, you dorks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have something you may be interested in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a partial date and one person's last name blackboxed. There are quite a few articles with low-to-no censorship, you just gotta look.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We have plenty of uncensored articles. Just browsing my stuff, I see lots. SCP-360, SCP-1684, and SCP-1673 all lack expungement. They don't even have blackboxes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've dabbled in using minimal expungement, redaction, and blackboxing in my articles before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are plenty of sparsely-expunged articles on the wiki. What are you talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is the only uncensored article on the wiki (the only thing being EXPUNGED/REDACTED/BLACK BOXXED being a name, which is standared procedure for article writing)…why do we not have more like this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first glance it seems like such a tired idea. What elevates this SCP is Addendum 010-2. Such a small thing, but it totally changed this into something great.

item.title: Re: Images
item.content:encoded: Actually the 3rd one is a Fallout 3 Slave Collar.

item.title: Re: Images
item.content:encoded: what quake 2 mod did you get the third one from?

item.title: Images
item.content:encoded:
Searching around a little I found several images that could work for the collars.
I hope this help a lilttle. Collars

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tab, if you want to try to do up an image for this one (or any other article, for that matter), post 'em/upload 'em to a sandbox page and then link 'em in the article's discussion thread. 'Cos yeah, a good picture can only help (that should probably be in the guides somewhere…).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little sad that this article is so obscure, yet so early on the mainlist… maybe a picture will help it get the upvotes it deserves? *wink wink nudge nudge*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a fairly decent article early on in the list, I'm surprised this doesn't have more upvotes.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Yup. R.U.R Refrence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: do i smell a R.U.R. reference? very nice… also corrected spelling on experiment.
